description_long: This object consists of two rectangular solar panels joined side by side, each framed with a metallic border and covered with a grid of photovoltaic cells. The surface is predominantly a dark blue-gray, with a regular pattern of lighter grid lines indicating the individual cells. One of the panels has a visible area of damage or burn mark, characterized by a dark, irregular patch with some exposed material beneath. The panels are encased in a rigid frame, likely made of aluminum, providing structural support and durability. The overall design is flat and thin, typical of solar modules intended for mounting on rooftops or other flat surfaces. No mounting hardware, cabling, or electrical inverter is visible in the provided views.
description: A pair of rectangular, blue-gray solar panels with a metallic frame and a visible burn mark on one panel. The panels are flat, grid-patterned, and joined side by side.
description_view_0: Front view showing both solar panels side by side, with a clear view of the grid pattern and a prominent burn mark on the right panel.
description_view_1: Angled view from the left, highlighting the panel surfaces and the depth of the metallic frame, with the burn mark partially visible.
description_view_2: Side profile view showing the thinness of the panels and the separation between them, with no surface details visible.
description_view_3: Angled view from the right, again showing the panel surfaces and the burn mark, with the frame's depth visible.
attribute_panel_orientation: flat, no tilt, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: no mounting structure visible; panels have an aluminum frame
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 2 panels, joined side by side
